{"key_event":"Algerian crisis and birth of the Fifth Republic","reasoning":"Civil society remained very strong through unions, parties, strikes, welfare claim-making, and mass mobilization, but emergency powers, colonial repression, and the new executive-centered constitution slightly reduced its checking power. State capacity was pulled down by Fourth Republic cabinet instability and colonial defeats, yet modernization, welfare administration, coercive expansion in Algeria, and de Gaulle’s 1958 constitutional settlement left the state somewhat stronger overall.","society_change":-0.2,"society_power":8.4,"state_change":0.2,"state_power":8.9}
